title: Intel Ends Work On Quantum Compiler Open-Source Bits - Phoronix
why:
- Intel has formally discontinued and archived its Quantum Passes open-source project, which provided additional compilation passes for the LLVM-based compiler in the Intel Quantum SDK.
- The Intel Quantum SDK is described as a "complete quantum computing stack" used for simulation and to develop code intended for future quantum hardware.
- The Quantum Passes project allowed developers to add custom compiler passes during the compilation process, which was integral to preparing quantum programs before targeting Intel quantum backends.
- The Quantum Passes code was last updated a year ago (with Quantum SDK 1.1.1 release) and now the project repository is read-only and no longer maintained or supported by Intel.
- This move follows a year without updates to the Intel Quantum SDK and is part of a broader pattern of Intel discontinuing open-source projects.
